page: Microsoft Azure L16as_v4 is a virtualized server instance belonging to the Lasv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz and includes 32 MB of L3 cache. The hardware configuration is bundled with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 3840 GB of Premium Storage capable capacity, while containing no GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. Text processing is a notable strength, ranking in the top 10% with a Geekbench text processing score of 2752.0. However, memory subsystem tradeoffs are present; while cached small-block read bandwidth is average at 1216.02 GB/sec, large-block uncached read bandwidth is weak, falling into the bottom 25% at 37.93 GB/sec. Multi-core execution across stress-ng and Geekbench remains in the middle 50%, as does LLM inference for medium-sized models, though prompt processing for large 70B models is poor at 4.44 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L16as_v4 offers a high local storage-to-vCPU density alongside a 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a resource-dense option for workloads requiring substantial local scratch space without the premium of dedicated GPU hardware. The combination of strong single-threaded execution and high local storage capacity makes this instance suitable for database hosting, text processing pipelines, and moderate-scale web serving. It is also applicable for small-scale language model inference, though it is constrained for larger parameter models due to memory bandwidth limitations and the lack of hardware acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure L16as_v4 is an x86_64 virtual server from the Lasv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 3840 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier text processing capabilities, though uncached large-block memory read bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. Multi-core execution and database operations perform at average levels. Given its high storage-to-vCPU density and balanced memory allocation, the L16as_v4 is suited for database hosting, text processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring significant local storage capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L16as_v4 is a Lasv4-family virtual server featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and 3840 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core and text processing performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L16as_v4 is a Lasv4 virtual server with 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3840 GB local storage, optimized for database and storage workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual server combining high-capacity local storage, strong single-core performance, and a balanced memory-to-core ratio for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 3.7 GHz clock speed
128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
3840 GB of Premium Storage capable local storage capacity
Strong single-core CPU and top-tier text processing benchmark performance
Tradeoff of weak uncached large-block memory read bandwidth
Suited for database hosting, text processing, and storage-heavy workloads
